vitest or node:test for a small library?
add coverage with a threshold that fails CI
configure vitest for this monorepo

add tests + coverage pls

tests are flaky, fix the test setup

make the CI matrix run on node 22 and 24

fix the package exports so imports resolve
cut a release
why does this function return undefined
review this PR's test quality
set up testing for a new typescript library
expected_behavior: recommends Vitest for anything non-trivial and node:test only for dependency-free pure-Node libraries, with the tradeoff stated
configures v8 coverage (AST-remapped accuracy) with a threshold that fails CI, not an informational report
TypeScript works without a transpile step (native Vitest handling or tsx loader for node:test)
adds test + coverage scripts and points at where CI wires them

cases.prompt: add react testing library to our vite app
expected_behavior: sets up Vitest with jsdom (or browser mode with the tradeoff explained) and RTL with user-event
shows a real render/screen/assert example against an actual component from the repo, not a toy
routes E2E flows to Playwright rather than stretching RTL

cases.prompt: our coverage number is high but bugs still ship - fix our testing approach
expected_behavior: explains coverage is a gap-finder, not a quality guarantee; recommends assertion quality and edge-case review over chasing percentage
suggests PR-delta coverage or mutation-testing style probes rather than raising the global threshold
avoids adding vanity thresholds that incentivize shallow tests
